title: ITU and Semiconductors: A Single-Axiom Foundation for Devices, Scaling, Beyond-CMOS, and the 2026-2040 Roadmap (v1.0.0)
description:
We apply the Information-Theoretic Unification (ITU) framework (Terada 2026, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20109210) to semiconductor transistors. The single ITU axiom δS = δ<K> governs the Landauer minimum bit-erasure energy, the Boltzmann subthreshold-swing tyranny, the 3D scaling progression (FinFET → GAAFET → CFET), and the beyond-CMOS device landscape, providing a unified foundation for the semiconductor industry roadmap.

This is Tier 1 paper #4, completing the ITU engineering rectangle: Quantum Computing (Tier 1 #1, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20139391) = computation, Machine Consciousness / ASI (Tier 1 #2, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20150501) = intelligence, Cryptography (Tier 1 #3, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20151059) = communication, and Semiconductors (this paper) = physical substrate.

Phase 55: Establishes the ITU foundation. Landauer's k_B T ln 2 limit and the 60 mV/decade Boltzmann tyranny are shown to be inevitable consequences of the thermal modular Hamiltonian K_A = H / k_B T. At 300 K the Landauer energy is 17.9 meV per bit. Moore + Koomey trends suggest ~2.5 decades of energy improvement remain before reaching the Landauer limit. Beating Boltzmann requires non-thermal K_A (tunneling, ferroelectric, photonic, spin).

Phase 56: The FinFET → GAAFET → CFET progression is derived as ITU η-maximisation - geometric maximisation of gate-channel coupling area per channel volume. Quantum WKB tunneling overwhelms ON/OFF distinction below 1 nm, ending the classical MOSFET concept. Sharvin (quantum point-contact) resistance becomes dominant below 3 nm contact area, quantised in units of h/(2e^2) = 12.9 kΩ.

Phase 57: Eight beyond-CMOS device classes (Si CMOS, GAAFET, TFET, NC-FET, Spin/MTJ, Photonic, Cryogenic CMOS, Memristor) are benchmarked on a unified ITU figure-of-merit. Photonic computing achieves FoM ~17,500× silicon CMOS, driven by hν » k_B T non-thermal K_A. No single winner emerges; application-specific heterogeneous SoCs (CMOS + photonic interconnect + spin memory + memristor AI) dominate the 2030s.

Phase 58: Synthesis into a 2026-2040 industry roadmap:

  • Logistic adoption curves for seven beyond-CMOS technologies (GAAFET 50% adoption 2027, photonic interconnect 2029)
  • Process node floor saturating at ~0.5 nm
  • Semiconductor TAM reaching $1 trillion by 2030, $2 trillion by 2040
  • Taiwan leading-edge share dropping 55% → 36% through geopolitical diversification (CHIPS Act, Rapidus, EU Chips Act)
  • 10 falsifiable predictions issued for 2026-2040 testing
  • Policy recommendations at national, corporate, and individual levels

Central thesis: under ITU, the transistor is a 1-bit QECC against k_B T noise; 3D wrapping geometry reflects K_A area maximisation; beyond-CMOS winners require non-thermal K_A. This completes the ITU engineering rectangle, with semiconductors providing the physical substrate on which Tier 1 #1-#3 are implemented.

Includes 4 theory documents, 4 Python numerical experiments, 4 figures, 4 JSON summaries, paper metadata. Total runtime ~30 seconds on a modern laptop. Honest framing: this is a Pass-1 interpretive paper that reframes known semiconductor physics within ITU; novel quantitative predictions distinguishing ITU from standard physics await Pass-2 follow-up work.
